Menu unrelated navigation text should not be retained.

Aerodynamic Forces

Aerodynamic forces are generated when air flows around a body. Lift and drag are components of the total aerodynamic force. The force depends on the shape of the object, the velocity of the flow, the density of the air, and the reference area used in the analysis.

Lift and Drag

Lift acts perpendicular to the flow direction while drag acts opposite the motion through the air. Pressure distribution and shear stress both contribute to the aerodynamic force. These statements are educational descriptions for learning about aerodynamics, not operational flight procedures for a specific aircraft.

Page Last Updated: May 13, 2024
